pullquote_provenance.speaker: Teamed Client Operations
pullquote_provenance.note: Anonymous-team voice per memory rule: Teamed Client Operations is the canonical anonymisation when no MI extraction supports a named-individual quote. Quote drafts the in-house view on PLAWA frontload-versus-accrue and the Chicago overlay; no MI provenance required because no individual is named.

prompt_corrections.note: Page-builder guidance gave $13,916 which is the 2025 wage base. Verified 2026 figure is $14,250 (a $334 YoY increase).

prompt_corrections.primary_source: Bloomberg Tax 'Illinois Releases 2026 Unemployment Insurance Tax Rates'
prompt_corrections.note: Page-builder guidance gave ~3.95% which appears to be an older/inflated figure. Verified 2026 default new-employer rate is 3.35% (3.45% for NAICS sector 56 administrative-support and waste-management).

prompt_corrections.prompt_value: Chicago has SEPARATE City of Chicago lease + amusement taxes (not income tax, but worth flagging for multi-city payroll).
prompt_corrections.verified_value: Reframed: Chicago Lease Transaction Tax and Amusement Tax are business-side, not payroll. The page-relevant Chicago overlays for payroll/HR are: (1) the Chicago Paid Leave and Paid Sick Leave Ordinance (dual 40+40 bucket, more generous than PLAWA), and (2) the Chicago minimum wage ($16.60/hour through 30 June 2026, with 1 July annual reset).
prompt_corrections.note: The actual payroll-relevant Chicago overlays are the dual-bucket Paid Leave Ordinance and the Chicago minimum wage, not the Lease/Amusement Tax. The lease/amusement taxes are noted in passing as a business-side levy but are not the central Chicago story for an EOR/payroll page.

factual_uncertainty_flags.note: Chicago minimum wage resets 1 July annually per CPI or 2.5%, whichever is lower. The $16.60 figure is valid only through 30 June 2026. The 1 July 2026 rate is not yet announced at time of writing — schedule a check in late Q2 2026.
16-day cure auto-expires 1 July 2026. Calendar entry recommended.
Each employer's specific experience-rated SUI rate must be verified against their own MyTax Illinois rate notice for accuracy; the 0.75-7.05% range is the system-wide band.

$2,925 verified per IDOR Q&A page; consistent with the 2025 base of $2,850. The 2026 figure may receive a late-year revision in FY 2026-15 bulletin updates — re-verify against IDOR publications page each January.

scheduled_data_update.action: Re-verify Chicago minimum wage after 1 July 2026 reset (currently $16.60; could rise per CPI ≤ 2.5% rule).
Update copy on the page to remove cure-period language after 1 July 2026 auto-expiry; verify whether OLS extends or supersedes the rule.
Re-verify IDES 2027 wage base, new-employer rate, and experience-rated range from the late-November 2026 EA-50 publication.
